asi: Added bolero fuzz state-machine for Registry asserting register-once atomicity + dead-reads-absent via a model-based oracle (per-name claim (id,incarnation), per-id liveness). Identity proven by a message round-trip through the registered slot's own receiver, NOT ActorId equality, because test_support::unstarted_actor assigns a fixed ActorId (all fuzzed actors share id 0). Directly targets #152 'fuzz over wrong surface': registry had unit concurrency tests but no fuzz. Found+fixed a modeling bug where the oracle asserted resolved.id()==ActorId::new(cid); corrected to a round-trip. checks.sh scoped to bombay-core --lib clippy to avoid pre-existing src/ (kameo oracle) nursery-lint violations pulled in by bench/test deps.

Added two proptest! property tests to bombay-core/src/registry.rs covering defensive-boundary name handling: register/lookup/unregister round-trip under arbitrary String names (empty/Unicode/up-to-512-char, the rubric's 'strings empty/max/max+1' mandate) and non-cross-contamination of distinct arbitrary names. Existing unit suite uses only short ASCII names; iter-1 bolero fuzz uses only u8 names. Fills the adversarial-string gap. Gate green.

Added bolero fuzz state-machine for the reply channel (reply_channel, #115/#118) asserting the exact outcome matrix: send->Ok(v), send_err->Err(Handler(e)), send-after-receiver-drop->AskerGone, recv-after-sender-drop->Interrupted. Reply was previously only proptested, never fuzzed -- broadens the #152 'fuzz over wrong surface' fix to the ask/reply surface. Runs recv on a current-thread tokio runtime (fuzz-only, like actor_loop target). Fixed compile issues: AskError lacks PartialEq (assert via match on variant+inner), match *op moved out of &Op (use match op + deref). Gate green.
